Krishna explains the immense scale of cosmic time, emphasizing that ordinary beings cannot perceive it. Those who understand it gain wisdom about creation and dissolution.
Cultivate awareness of the vastness of life and universe to gain perspective and reduce attachment to temporary things.
All beings, manifested or unmanifested, emerge from the unmanifest; at the dissolution of night, they merge back into the unmanifest, the primal state.
All creation emerges from and returns to the unmanifested supreme reality.
The cycle of creation and dissolution is rooted in the eternal unmanifested reality.
Krishna teaches that the universe manifests from the unmanifested (Brahman) and dissolves back into it. Understanding this cycle helps one grasp the transient nature of material existence.
See life’s events as temporary manifestations; focus on connecting with the eternal essence within.
This cosmic creation, O Arjuna, comes into being and dissolves repeatedly; inevitably, at the end of night, it manifests again.
The world continuously cycles through creation and dissolution.
The universe operates in rhythmic, predictable cycles.
Krishna describes the inevitability of creation and dissolution, highlighting the eternal law governing the universe. Understanding this encourages detachment from fleeting worldly concerns.
Accept life’s changes with equanimity, knowing all experiences arise and pass in cosmic rhythm.
Beyond this, there is another reality, unmanifested, eternal; though it perishes in beings, it never truly dies.
The eternal essence pervades all beings, remaining indestructible.
While forms perish, the eternal reality within is indestructible.
Krishna explains that the unmanifested essence, though appearing to perish in living beings, is immortal and ever-present. Recognizing this helps one transcend fear of death.
Focus on the eternal aspect of life within you and others; cultivate detachment from transient forms.
The unmanifested, imperishable, is declared as the supreme goal. Those who reach it never return; that is My supreme abode.
The eternal, unchanging reality is the highest destination, beyond rebirth.
Attaining the unmanifested leads to permanent liberation.
Krishna describes the unmanifested (Avyakta) as eternal and imperishable. Devotees who attain this supreme state escape the cycle of birth and death and reside in His ultimate abode.
Focus on spiritual practice to connect with the eternal essence, transcending temporary worldly experiences.
The supreme person, O Partha, is accessible through exclusive devotion; within Him dwell all beings, and by Him all this universe is pervaded.
God is omnipresent and approachable through devoted love.
Exclusive devotion allows one to perceive God as the inner essence of all beings.
Krishna emphasizes that the divine pervades every being. Those devoted to Him with single-minded love recognize the unity of all creation within the supreme Self.
Practice seeing the divine in all beings and approach life with love and devotion.
O best among Bharatas, I shall describe the time (Kala) by which yogis, departing at proper moments, reach liberation.
Timing of departure from the body influences spiritual attainment.
Awareness of cosmic and personal timing aids yogis in achieving liberation.
Krishna speaks of “Kala,” the cosmic principle governing life and death. Yogis who leave the body in alignment with these principles reach higher states without rebirth.
Cultivate mindfulness of life’s rhythms and practice yoga consistently to align with higher cosmic order.
By Agni, light, day, the bright half of the lunar month, and the six months of Uttarayana, those who depart reach Brahman; the wise attain liberation.
Departing in auspicious periods leads to spiritual progress.
Certain cosmic periods favor liberation for disciplined souls.

Krishna explains that leaving the body during favorable cosmic phases (like Uttarayana or the bright half of the month) helps yogis attain the supreme reality. Knowledge and devotion align with cosmic rhythms.
Respect natural and cosmic rhythms; practice spirituality in harmony with auspicious timings.
By smoke, night, the dark half of the lunar month, and the six months of Dakshinayana, the yogi departs and returns to the moon or subtle light, resuming the cycle.
Departing during inauspicious times leads to continuation in the cycle of rebirth.
Timing and awareness of one’s departure from life influence spiritual outcome.

Krishna indicates that leaving the body during less auspicious periods results in returning to lower states of existence. Yogis must cultivate awareness and discipline to transcend the cycle of birth.
Live consciously and cultivate spiritual awareness to maximize the opportunity for liberation.
These paths of the bright and dark halves of the lunar month are considered eternal; one follows the unmanifested path, the other returns again.
Creation and dissolution follow cyclical patterns, with paths either leading to liberation or rebirth.
Understanding cosmic cycles helps the yogi choose the right spiritual path.
Krishna explains that the universe follows a consistent rhythm: some paths lead to liberation (unmanifested), others return to the cycle of birth. Recognizing these helps the yogi act wisely.
Study and align with spiritual and cosmic laws to progress toward liberation.
O Partha, no one is bewildered in all these times by a yogi who is devoted to Me; therefore, be ever-yogic.
A devoted yogi is never confused or lost.
Consistent devotion ensures clarity, stability, and guidance in life.
Krishna assures that a yogi who remains devoted and disciplined is never deluded, regardless of external circumstances or cosmic cycles. Devotion brings wisdom and stability.
Maintain consistent devotion and yoga practice to navigate life with clarity and confidence.
O yogi, knowing all the auspicious results of Vedic rituals, sacrifices, austerities, and charity, surpass them all by reaching the supreme position through yoga.
Yoga surpasses ritualistic merit and leads directly to the supreme.
Inner spiritual discipline (yoga) is more effective than external rituals alone.
Krishna teaches that while rituals, austerities, and charity bring merit, yoga—the disciplined union with the divine—elevates one beyond worldly rewards to the highest spiritual state.
Focus on inner spiritual development through yoga and devotion rather than only external religious practices.

chapter 9.EXPLAINATIONS: Translation
The Blessed Lord said: I shall now reveal to you, who are without envy, this most secret knowledge, combined with wisdom, by knowing which you shall be freed from evil.
Short Explanation
Krishna introduces the supreme secret of knowledge that liberates from sin and suffering.
Key Takeaway
Spiritual knowledge with wisdom leads to liberation.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna addresses Arjuna, promising to teach the most confidential knowledge—the essence of the divine truth, combined with spiritual insight. Knowing this brings freedom from ignorance, attachment, and the cycle of suffering.
Life Application
Seek deeper understanding of spiritual truths with humility and devotion for inner freedom.
Translation
This is the king of sciences, the most secret of secrets, the supreme purifier, directly perceivable, harmonious with dharma, joyous, and imperishable.
Short Explanation
True spiritual knowledge is supreme, sacred, and liberating.
Key Takeaway
The highest knowledge is both experiential and transformative.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna emphasizes that this knowledge is not theoretical but experiential, guiding one to live in alignment with dharma and experience enduring joy. It surpasses all worldly learning in purity and permanence.
Life Application
Focus on learning and practicing spiritual knowledge rather than mere intellectual study.
Translation
Those who are faithless, O Arjuna, do not attain Me; they return after death in the cycle of birth and death.
Short Explanation
Faith and devotion are essential to attain God; without them, one remains bound in the world.
Key Takeaway
Belief and trust in divine knowledge are prerequisites for liberation.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna notes that only those with sincere faith can reach the divine. The faithless remain trapped in the samsara of life and death, unable to attain ultimate freedom.
Life Application
Cultivate unwavering faith and trust in divine guidance.
Translation
By Me, this entire universe is pervaded in an unmanifest form. All beings reside in Me, but I am not in them.
Short Explanation
Krishna pervades all beings without being limited to them.
Key Takeaway
The divine is immanent in all yet transcends all.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains His omnipresence: though all beings exist within Him, He is not confined by them. This distinction between immanence and transcendence helps the devotee understand the nature of the divine.
Life Application
See the presence of God in all, while realizing the divine also transcends worldly limitations.
Translation
Nor do the beings dwell in Me, but My Self resides in them; I am the origin of all beings.
Short Explanation
Krishna is the inner Self of all beings.
Key Takeaway
God is both the source and indweller of all creation.
Detailed Explanation
While the universe exists in Him in potential, Krishna dwells within each being as the inner essence, guiding, sustaining, and pervading all. Recognizing this inner presence strengthens devotion.
Life Application
Meditate on the divine presence within yourself and others to develop reverence and compassion.
Translation
Just as the mighty wind pervades the space, so do all beings pervade Me.
Short Explanation
The divine pervades all creation effortlessly, like air in space.
Key Takeaway
God’s omnipresence is natural and all-encompassing.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna uses the analogy of wind and space to explain that all beings exist in the divine without limiting or containing Him. This illustrates the omnipresence of God in a simple, accessible way.
Life Application
Recognize the divine presence in every moment and every being around you.
Translation
All beings, O Kaunteya, enter into My nature; at the end of a cosmic cycle, I release them, and again at the beginning, I create them.
Short Explanation
The universe cycles through creation and dissolution under God’s guidance.
Key Takeaway
God orchestrates the eternal rhythm of creation and dissolution.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna explains that all beings operate under His will, moving through cycles of birth and rebirth. He creates and dissolves the universe according to cosmic time, maintaining balance and order.
Life Application
Understand that life is part of a larger cosmic rhythm; accept changes with faith in divine order.
Translation
I continuously create nature and the entire manifest world under My control; all beings are inevitably subject to this nature.
Short Explanation
The world exists under the influence of divine nature.
Key Takeaway
Understanding the role of nature helps one act wisely and without attachment.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna describes the ongoing creation process: all beings are subject to the forces of Prakriti (nature), and He oversees this cosmic play. Recognizing this allows one to act with detachment and clarity.
Life Application
Accept natural laws and responsibilities while maintaining inner balance and detachment.
Translation
O Dhananjaya, these actions do not bind Me; I am indifferent, detached, and unaffected by them.
Short Explanation
The divine remains unattached despite being the source of all action.
Key Takeaway
God’s detachment is perfect; we can emulate detachment in our actions.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna emphasizes that although all actions occur within Him, He remains untouched, unaffected, and uninvolved in binding karma. This detachment provides a model for yogis to perform duties without attachment.
Life Application
Perform your duties with focus and dedication but without attachment to outcomes.
Translation
Under My direction, nature produces all moving and non-moving beings. By this interplay of causes, O Kaunteya, the world revolves.
Short Explanation
Nature acts under divine guidance, creating and sustaining the universe.
Key Takeaway
God governs the universe through nature and causes; understanding this reduces fear and confusion.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna describes how He directs nature to create all beings and phenomena. This interplay of cause and effect sustains the world and demonstrates the divine orchestration behind existence.
Life Application
Trust in the divine order and act responsibly, knowing that your efforts are part of a greater cosmic plan.
Translation
Fools, who do not know Me, consider Me to be embodied in a human form. They do not know My supreme, imperishable nature as the Lord of all beings.
Short Explanation
Those who perceive God only as a human miss His divine, all-pervading essence.
Key Takeaway
True understanding of God recognizes His supreme, eternal nature beyond human form.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains that many are deluded, perceiving Him merely as a human. Ignorant of His transcendental form, they fail to grasp His omnipotence and omnipresence. Recognizing His divine essence is the key to true devotion.
Life Application
Cultivate awareness of God’s all-pervading, eternal presence beyond physical forms.
Translation
Those of empty desires, empty actions, and empty knowledge, whose minds are deluded, are endowed with a demonic and illusory nature.
Short Explanation
People driven by ignorance, ego, and material desires are led by demonic tendencies.
Key Takeaway
Uncontrolled desires and ignorance lead to suffering and illusion.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna warns that those who act without understanding, chasing selfish desires or performing meaningless rituals, fall under the influence of delusion and harmful tendencies. This contrasts with the divine path of wisdom and devotion.
Life Application
Examine motives and knowledge behind your actions to avoid harmful habits and illusions.
Translation
But the great souls, O Partha, who are devoted to Me and take refuge in My divine nature, know Me as the imperishable source of all beings and worship Me with single-minded devotion.
Short Explanation
Sincere devotees recognize God as the eternal source of all life.
Key Takeaway
Devotion to the eternal essence of God leads to wisdom and liberation.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna distinguishes between deluded and noble souls: the great, devoted souls align their hearts with Him, understanding His eternal nature and worshipping with undivided focus, leading to spiritual perfection.
Life Application
Cultivate single-minded devotion and seek the eternal aspect of the divine in your spiritual practice.
Translation
Constantly praising Me, striving earnestly, and steadfast in vows, offering salutations, they worship Me with devotion, ever united with Me.
Short Explanation
Steadfast, consistent devotion manifests in praise, discipline, and heartfelt worship.
Key Takeaway
Consistent devotion and discipline strengthen spiritual connection with God.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna emphasizes that devotion is an active, continuous process: praising, following vows, and offering salutations all contribute to remaining connected with the divine.
Life Application
Engage in regular devotional practices and maintain spiritual discipline to deepen your connection with God.
Translation
Some, through the sacrifice of knowledge, also worship Me. Some worship Me in unity; some worship Me in diversity, facing all directions of the universe.
Short Explanation
Different forms of worship—through knowledge or rituals—lead to connection with God.
Key Takeaway
The divine can be approached through multiple paths: knowledge, devotion, or rituals.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna explains that worship can be singularly focused or multiple, through knowledge or ritual. The sincerity of devotion, rather than form, is what connects the worshipper to God.
Life Application
Respect diverse paths of devotion, focusing on sincerity and understanding in your practice.
Translation
I am the ritual, the sacrifice, the offering, the medicinal herb; I am the mantra, the oblation, the fire, and the offering in every sacrifice.
Short Explanation
Krishna is the essence behind all rituals and spiritual acts.
Key Takeaway
God is the substance of all sacred actions and rituals.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna teaches that all acts of worship—rituals, offerings, or chants—are manifestations of Him. Recognizing His presence in every sacred act transforms ordinary practice into spiritual devotion.
Life Application
Perform rituals and spiritual duties with awareness of God as their essence.
Translation
I am the father of this world, its mother, the supporter, the grandsire. I am the sacred syllable Om, the Vedas, the sacrifice, and the ultimate goal.
Short Explanation
Krishna is the origin, sustainer, and purpose of all existence.
Key Takeaway
God encompasses creation, knowledge, and the ultimate spiritual goal.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna declares His omnipresence and omnipotence: He is the source and sustainer of all, guiding beings through creation, knowledge, and spiritual practices.
Life Application
See God as the source of life, wisdom, and liberation in all aspects of existence.
Translation
I am the goal, the sustainer, the lord, the witness, the abode, the refuge, the friend; I am creation, dissolution, sustenance, the place of rest, the imperishable seed.
Short Explanation
Krishna is the ultimate in all roles: creator, preserver, witness, and protector.
Key Takeaway
God is the ultimate in every aspect of existence.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna outlines His omnipresence in worldly and spiritual functions, showing that He is the ultimate authority, witness, and protector. Knowing this leads to devotion and surrender.
Life Application
Trust in God as your guide, refuge, and ultimate support in all life situations.
Translation
I sustain all beings for a year, control them, and release them. I am immortal, death, being, non-being, O Arjuna.
Short Explanation
Krishna governs life, death, and the cycle of existence.
Key Takeaway
God is the controller of all temporal and eternal realities.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna reveals His control over life and death, immortality, and the cycles of existence. Recognition of this divine management encourages surrender and faith.
Life Application
Accept life and death as expressions of divine will, cultivating detachment and devotion.
Translation
Those who, drinking soma, or performing Vedic rituals, seek heaven through sacrifices, attain heavenly pleasures. After enjoying divine bliss, they return to the mortal world.
Short Explanation
Merit from rituals and sacrifices leads to temporary heavenly rewards.
Key Takeaway
Worldly rituals bring temporary results; ultimate liberation requires devotion to God.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna explains that while rituals and sacrifices yield temporary enjoyment in heavenly realms, they do not grant eternal liberation. Devotion and knowledge of Him are required for lasting freedom.
Life Application
Engage in rituals with awareness but focus on devotion and knowledge for eternal spiritual progress.
Translation
Having enjoyed the vast pleasures of heaven and exhausted their merit, beings return to the mortal world. Thus, those who follow the threefold duties attain what they desire.
Short Explanation
Heavenly pleasures are temporary; actions aligned with dharma bring fulfillment but not permanent liberation.
Key Takeaway
Worldly and even heavenly rewards are temporary; devotion and dharma guide toward lasting fulfillment.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains that souls who enjoy heavenly rewards eventually return to the mortal plane after the merit of their actions is exhausted. Those following dharma and spiritual practices achieve what they desire, but only devotion ensures permanent liberation.
Life Application
Focus on devotion and spiritual discipline rather than transient rewards, even those in heavenly realms.
Translation
But those who worship Me with exclusive devotion, thinking of Me constantly, I carry the burden of their welfare and protection.
Short Explanation
Exclusive, constant devotion places the devotee under God’s care.
Key Takeaway
Sincere, unwavering devotion ensures divine protection and guidance.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna promises that devotees who focus on Him constantly need not fear worldly or spiritual challenges. He personally ensures their well-being, safeguarding them from harm and guiding them toward liberation.
Life Application
Practice constant remembrance and devotion to God for protection and spiritual support.
Translation
Even those who worship other gods with faith, offering sacrifices with devotion, actually worship Me, O Kaunteya, performing them according to proper ritual.
Short Explanation
All sincere devotion ultimately reaches God, regardless of the form of worship.
Key Takeaway
God accepts all sincere devotion directed with faith, even if expressed differently.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna clarifies that worship of other deities with genuine devotion is, in essence, worship of Him. The intention and faith behind devotion matter more than the external form.
Life Application
Respect diverse devotional practices, focusing on sincerity and faith rather than external forms.
Translation
I am the enjoyer and Lord of all sacrifices. But those who do not know My true nature become lost.
Short Explanation
God is the ultimate recipient of all devotion and offerings. Ignorance leads to loss of spiritual benefit.
Key Takeaway
Understanding God’s supreme nature ensures that devotion leads to liberation.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains that He is the ultimate enjoyer of all rituals and sacrifices. Without recognizing His supreme essence, devotees may perform acts without attaining spiritual gain. Knowledge of God’s true nature is essential.
Life Application
Study and understand the divine essence behind rituals to make your actions spiritually fruitful.
Translation
Those who follow the path of gods go to the gods, those who follow the path of ancestors go to the ancestors. All beings go according to their worship, but My devotees come to Me.
Short Explanation
The destination of the soul is determined by its focus of worship.
Key Takeaway
Devotion to God leads to liberation, while other paths lead to temporary destinations.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains that the spiritual destination depends on one’s devotion. Worshippers of deities, ancestors, or other entities reach corresponding realms temporarily. Only those devoted to Him reach the supreme, eternal abode.
Life Application
Direct your devotion toward God for lasting spiritual attainment.
Translation
Whoever offers Me a leaf, a flower, fruit, or water with devotion, I accept it offered by the pure-hearted.
Short Explanation
Even small offerings given with devotion are accepted by God.
Key Takeaway
The sincerity of devotion matters more than the size or value of the offering.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna emphasizes that purity of heart and devotion in offering—even the simplest items—transforms them into acts of true worship, connecting the devotee with the divine.
Life Application
Offer your actions, however small, with love and devotion for maximum spiritual impact.
Translation
Whatever you do, eat, offer in sacrifice, give as charity, or practice austerity, O Kaunteya, do it as an offering to Me.
Short Explanation
Every action becomes spiritually fruitful when offered to God.
Key Takeaway
Life itself can become a continuous act of devotion through conscious offering.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna teaches that dedicating all actions—whether mundane or sacred—to Him purifies them, freeing the devotee from the binding effects of karma.
Life Application
Approach daily activities as offerings to God, turning routine life into spiritual practice.
Translation
In this way, you shall be freed from the bondage of action, both good and bad. A person devoted and united in yoga attains liberation and comes to Me.
Short Explanation
Yoga and devotion free one from the results of all actions.
Key Takeaway
Spiritual discipline and devotion lead to liberation beyond karmic bonds.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna explains that offering all actions to Him while remaining devoted dissolves the binding effects of both meritorious and sinful deeds. Such a yogi, united in devotion, attains liberation and reaches God.
Life Application
Practice yoga and devote all actions to God to transcend karma and achieve spiritual freedom.
Translation
I am equally disposed to all beings; I am neither envious nor partial. But those who worship Me with devotion, they are in Me, and I am also in them.
Short Explanation
God treats all beings equally, yet maintains a special bond with His devotees.
Key Takeaway
Devotion creates a reciprocal, loving connection with God.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna emphasizes impartiality in the world, yet those who engage in sincere devotion enter into a mutual, intimate relationship with the divine. This bond ensures spiritual growth and protection.
Life Application
Maintain respect and love for all beings while cultivating a devoted relationship with God.
Translation
Even if a person of the worst conduct worships Me with exclusive devotion, he should be considered a righteous person; for he is rightly disposed.
Short Explanation
Sincere devotion transforms even the most flawed individual.
Key Takeaway
True devotion outweighs past mistakes; God values the heart’s intent.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna assures that even those who have erred greatly can attain righteousness through single-minded devotion. What matters is the focus and sincerity of their heart, not prior actions.
Life Application
Approach God with full devotion and sincerity, knowing that genuine faith transforms character and destiny.
Translation
Quickly becomes righteous, and attains eternal peace. O Kaunteya, know for certain that My devotee never perishes.
Short Explanation
Devotion guarantees spiritual protection and eternal peace.
Key Takeaway
Sincere devotion ensures that the devotee is never lost or destroyed.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna assures Arjuna that anyone devoted to Him attains inner peace and righteousness rapidly. Devotees, by aligning themselves with the divine, transcend worldly dangers and karmic consequences.
Life Application
Cultivate steady devotion to God to achieve inner tranquility and security in life.
Translation
Even those who take refuge in Me, O Partha, though born in sinful families, such as women, Vaishyas, and Shudras, attain the supreme goal.
Short Explanation
Devotion transcends birth, gender, and social background.
Key Takeaway
God’s grace is available to all sincere devotees, regardless of worldly identity.
Detailed Explanation
Krishna emphasizes that sincere devotion is not limited by caste, gender, or social status. Those who worship Him faithfully, even if considered sinful or low-born, reach the supreme abode.
Life Application
Focus on devotion and faith rather than social labels; all can attain spiritual liberation through sincere worship.
Translation
How much more so the Brahmins, the righteous, the devotees, and the royal sages. Although this world is impermanent and full of sorrow, worship Me upon attaining it.
Short Explanation
Sages and righteous beings, by devoting themselves, transcend worldly suffering.
Key Takeaway
All who recognize life’s impermanence should devote themselves to God.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna notes that while the world is temporary and often filled with suffering, those who are wise and righteous focus their devotion on Him. Even brief recognition of God in this fleeting world leads to liberation.
Life Application
Reflect on the impermanence of worldly life and dedicate yourself to spiritual practice and devotion.
Translation
Be devoted to Me, let your mind be absorbed in Me, be My devotee, offer Me worship, and bow to Me. By thus being united with Me, you shall come to Me alone, O Arjuna.
Short Explanation
Complete devotion, worship, and surrender lead directly to God.
Key Takeaway
Single-minded devotion and surrender are the surest paths to divine union.
Detailed Explanation

Krishna instructs Arjuna to cultivate constant remembrance, devotion, and worship. By offering one’s mind, body, and actions to God, and surrendering fully, one attains unity with the divine. This summarizes the essence of Bhakti Yoga.
Life Application
Center your thoughts and actions on God through devotion, worship, and surrender to achieve spiritual fulfillment.

chapter 10.EXPLAINATION: The Blessed Lord said: Listen once more, O mighty-armed Arjuna, to My supreme word, which I speak out of love for you and for your welfare.
Krishna prepares to reveal His most beloved teachings for Arjuna’s benefit.
Divine knowledge is shared out of love and guidance for the sincere seeker.

Krishna begins Chapter 10 by signaling that the following teachings are precious, meant for Arjuna’s spiritual growth. These words are the essence of divine wisdom, imparted with love and concern for his highest good.
Approach divine teachings with an open heart and readiness to absorb their wisdom.
Neither the gods nor the great sages know My origin. I am the source of all; I am the beginning of the gods and the sages, in every respect.
Krishna is the eternal origin of all, beyond the knowledge of even gods and sages.
The divine source transcends all beings, including celestial and enlightened ones.

Krishna emphasizes His supreme position: He is the origin of all creation, even the gods and the great sages. This illustrates the concept of God as the ultimate, all-encompassing source beyond ordinary comprehension.
Recognize God as the ultimate source of all existence and honor the divine in all forms.
He who knows Me as unborn, beginningless, the Lord of all worlds, and is not deluded, is freed from all sins among mortals.
Knowledge of God’s eternal, unborn nature leads to liberation from sin.
Spiritual realization of God’s true nature purifies and liberates the soul.
Krishna asserts that understanding His eternal, uncreated nature as the supreme ruler of all worlds liberates one from karmic bondage. Such insight removes delusion and grants spiritual freedom.
Seek to understand the eternal and unchanging essence of God to cultivate liberation from worldly bondage.

Intelligence, knowledge, freedom from delusion, forgiveness, truthfulness, self-restraint, tranquility, happiness, unhappiness, being and non-being, fear and fearlessness — these are all aspects of Me.
All qualities and experiences in the world are manifestations of God.
Recognizing God in all aspects of life fosters awareness and devotion.
Krishna lists His manifestations in every facet of life, including mental qualities, emotional states, and existential experiences. This teaches that all reality is pervaded by the divine essence.
Develop awareness that God manifests in all qualities and experiences, cultivating equanimity and devotion.
Non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame, and infamy — these exist in beings according to their connection with Me.
All virtues and actions of beings arise from their relationship with God.
Divine connection influences all behavior and qualities in life.

Krishna explains that qualities like patience, charity, fame, or shame in beings are expressions of His divine energy. Understanding this helps one see the divine influence in all actions and outcomes.
Act with virtue and devotion, understanding that your qualities are guided by divine energy.
The seven great sages and the four Manus, and all those born with devotion to Me in mind — through them this world is created.
God manifests creation through sages and devoted souls.
Divine presence directs creation through enlightened and devoted beings.
Krishna highlights the role of sages and devoted individuals in the divine orchestration of the universe. Those who are devoted carry His essence and help maintain cosmic order.
Respect wisdom and devotion in others, recognizing them as channels of divine energy.
He who truly knows My divine manifestations and My yoga becomes steadfast in yoga without doubt.
Realization of God’s manifestations leads to unwavering spiritual practice.
Knowledge of the divine strengthens faith, focus, and yogic discipline.
Krishna teaches that understanding His various manifestations helps the devotee maintain firm faith and practice yoga steadily, without confusion or doubt.
Study the divine presence in the world to strengthen your spiritual discipline and confidence.
I am the origin of all; everything proceeds from Me. Those who understand this worship Me with a mind absorbed in devotion.
Recognizing God as the origin of all inspires devotion and worship.
Understanding the divine source encourages sincere devotion.
Krishna emphasizes that the knowledge of Him as the origin of all beings inspires wisdom and devotion. The wise, aware of this truth, worship Him with deep reverence.
Meditate on God as the source of all existence and cultivate heartfelt worship.
Fixing their minds on Me, with their lives dedicated to Me, they enlighten each other, speak of Me constantly, delight in Me, and rejoice in Me.
Devotion manifests in thought, speech, and mutual encouragement among devotees.
Sharing devotion and knowledge strengthens spiritual community and joy.
Krishna describes the active life of devotees who are united in thought and speech, sharing the divine message and rejoicing in it. Mutual devotion amplifies spiritual growth.
Engage with like-minded spiritual companions, sharing devotion and insights to strengthen your practice.
To those ever devoted to Me with love, I grant the understanding by which they come to Me.
God provides wisdom to devotees that leads directly to Him.
Devotion is rewarded with divine guidance toward ultimate union with God.
Krishna concludes this section by affirming that love and devotion are the means by which He grants knowledge. This wisdom leads the devotee to spiritual realization and union with the divine.
Cultivate loving devotion; it is the key to receiving divine guidance and reaching God.
Out of compassion for them, I, dwelling in the self, destroy the darkness born of ignorance with the luminous lamp of knowledge.
Krishna dispels ignorance and guides devotees toward enlightenment.
Divine knowledge removes the darkness of ignorance in the heart.

Krishna explains that His presence illuminates the mind and soul of devotees. By offering knowledge and wisdom, He destroys the spiritual darkness that binds beings to ignorance, fostering clarity and liberation.
Seek divine guidance and cultivate wisdom to overcome ignorance in your life.
Arjuna said: You are the Supreme Brahman, the supreme abode, pure and transcendental, eternal, divine, the primal God, and unborn.
Arjuna praises Krishna as the eternal, divine source of all.
Recognizing God’s supreme, eternal nature deepens devotion and understanding.

Arjuna acknowledges Krishna’s transcendence, purity, and divine origin. This understanding forms the foundation for Bhakti Yoga, where devotion is grounded in the awareness of God as the ultimate reality.
Meditate on God’s eternal and divine qualities to strengthen your spiritual focus.
All the sages, the divine seers, and even Narada, Asita, Devala, Vyasa, and others have declared You as such. I myself also speak of You, O Keshava.
Krishna’s supreme nature is recognized by all great sages and seers.
Scriptural and sage testimony confirms God’s supreme divinity.

Arjuna cites revered sages to affirm Krishna’s supremacy. This verse emphasizes that knowledge of the divine has been consistently recognized by enlightened beings, underscoring Krishna’s supreme authority.
Respect and study the teachings of sages to understand and connect with the divine.
I consider all this you say to be true, O Keshava. Indeed, neither the gods nor the demons know Your full manifestations.
God’s divine manifestations are beyond even celestial beings’ complete comprehension.
The infinite aspects of God transcend ordinary and divine understanding.
Arjuna recognizes that Krishna’s divine manifestations are limitless. Even gods and demons cannot fully comprehend His true, all-pervading nature, highlighting the supreme mystery of God.
Accept that God’s full nature is beyond human or divine grasp and cultivate devotion and humility.
You yourself know Yourself, O Supreme Person. You are the creator of beings, the Lord of all beings, the God of gods, and the Lord of the universe.
Krishna is self-aware, omniscient, and the supreme creator and ruler.
God is both the self-existent source and sovereign of all creation.
Arjuna acknowledges Krishna as the ultimate self-aware divine, manifesting as the creator and ruler of all beings. Recognizing God’s omnipotence and omnipresence strengthens faith and devotion.
Contemplate God’s all-encompassing authority to develop reverence and trust in His guidance
You are fit to describe all Your divine manifestations in full, O Lord of the universe, which pervade all the worlds in every direction.
Krishna’s divine manifestations pervade everything; He can be fully described.
Understanding God’s manifestations deepens spiritual insight and devotion.
Arjuna acknowledges Krishna’s ability to manifest divinity everywhere and to explain it fully. These manifestations inspire awe and encourage devotees to explore and contemplate divine qualities.
Study and reflect on God’s manifestations in the world to strengthen devotion.
How can I, who constantly meditates on You, fully understand Your infinite divine manifestations, O Bhagavan? In which forms do You dwell and reveal Yourself?
Arjuna seeks deeper understanding of Krishna’s infinite manifestations.
Curiosity and inquiry about the divine are integral to spiritual growth.
Arjuna expresses his wonder at Krishna’s infinite forms and presence in the world. Even devoted meditation leaves room for inquiry, emphasizing that understanding God is a lifelong pursuit.
Cultivate devotion alongside inquiry to deepen knowledge of God’s infinite nature.
Describe in detail Your divine manifestations and Your yoga, O Janardana. Hearing this fully brings satisfaction, as nothing else can provide such bliss.
Arjuna requests a detailed explanation of Krishna’s divine manifestations and yoga.
Learning about God’s manifestations and cosmic order brings joy and fulfillment.

Arjuna expresses that a full account of Krishna’s manifestations and divine yoga is the highest source of bliss. The verse emphasizes that understanding God’s greatness provides spiritual satisfaction surpassing all worldly pleasures.
Seek detailed knowledge of divine principles to enrich devotion and spiritual understanding.
The Blessed Lord said: I shall now describe to you My divine manifestations, O best of the Kurus. There is no end to My opulences.
Krishna begins describing His infinite divine manifestations.
God’s glory and manifestations are limitless; devotion to Him is ever-expanding.

Krishna initiates the enumeration of His divine manifestations, highlighting their infinite nature. Devotees are encouraged to contemplate these manifestations to deepen devotion and spiritual knowledge.
Meditate on and appreciate the limitless glory of God to strengthen faith and devotion.
I am the self, O Gudakesha, seated in the hearts of all beings. I am the beginning, the middle, and the end of all beings.
Krishna pervades all beings, as their inner self and the source, sustainer, and end of life.
God is the inner essence of all living beings and the ultimate reality of existence.
Krishna declares that He dwells within all beings, guiding and sustaining them. Recognizing God as the inner self leads to devotion, self-realization, and understanding of the universe’s order.
Reflect on God as the inner self of all beings and the ultimate source and goal of life to cultivate devotion and awareness.
Among the Adityas I am Vishnu, among lights the radiant Sun; among the Maruts I am Marichi, and among the stars, the Moon.
Krishna identifies Himself with the most prominent beings and celestial bodies.
God manifests in the highest and most influential forms in the universe.
Krishna demonstrates that His divine presence pervades all cosmic entities. By identifying with deities and celestial bodies, He shows that everything exalted in the universe reflects His glory.
Recognize God’s presence in the power and beauty of nature and cosmic forces.
Among the Vedas I am the Sama Veda, among gods I am Indra, among senses I am the mind, and among living beings I am consciousness.
Krishna embodies knowledge, leadership, intellect, and awareness.
Divine manifestations include knowledge, leadership, and consciousness.

Krishna highlights that He is the essence of sacred knowledge (Sama Veda), authority among gods (Indra), mental faculties (mind), and the life force (consciousness) within beings, demonstrating His omnipresence and omnipotence.
Cultivate awareness of God in knowledge, decision-making, and inner consciousness.
Among the Rudras I am Shiva, among Yakshas and Rakshasas I am Kubera; among Vasus I am Agni, and among mountains I am Meru.
Krishna pervades divine powers, wealth, fire, and the highest mountains.
God is reflected in strength, prosperity, and the world’s lofty aspects.
Krishna shows that His presence permeates gods of power, guardian spirits, elemental forces, and the highest mountains, illustrating His universal essence that governs all realms.
See God’s manifestation in strength, prosperity, and the majesty of the natural world.
Among the priests I am Brihaspati, among generals I am Skanda, and among rivers I am the ocean.
God manifests as wisdom, leadership, and the grandeur of nature.
Divine manifestations exist in guidance, leadership, and natural abundance.
Krishna emphasizes His presence in knowledge (Brihaspati), martial prowess (Skanda), and vast natural resources (ocean), highlighting that His essence permeates both human and natural realms.
Respect divine wisdom, leadership, and nature as reflections of God’s glory.
Among the great sages I am Bhrigu, among speech I am the single syllable Om, among sacrifices I am the chanting sacrifice, and among immovable things I am Mount Himalaya.
Krishna identifies with sages, sacred sound, rituals, and monumental nature.
God manifests in spiritual authority, sacredness, and enduring elements of creation.
Krishna explains that His presence is inherent in spiritual knowledge, divine sound, sacred rites, and towering natural wonders, showing that everything spiritually significant originates from Him.
Honor wisdom, sacred rituals, and enduring natural monuments as expressions of God.
Among trees I am the sacred Ashvattha, among divine sages I am Narada; among Gandharvas I am Chitraratha, and among Siddhas I am the sage Kapila.
Krishna’s divine manifestations exist in trees, sages, celestial musicians, and enlightened beings.
God is present in nature, spirituality, music, and wisdom.
Krishna shows that sacred trees, celestial beings, and enlightened sages embody His divine energy. These manifestations encourage reverence for spiritual and natural life.
See divinity in spiritual teachers, nature, and arts as reflections of God.
Among horses I am Uccaihsravas, among elephants I am Airavata, and among men I am the King of men.
Krishna manifests in nobility, strength, and leadership.
God’s presence is reflected in excellence and leadership in all realms.
Krishna highlights His presence in exceptional creatures like divine horses and elephants, and in human leadership, demonstrating that the supreme divinity is mirrored in exemplary beings.
Recognize excellence, leadership, and strength in life as expressions of God.
Among weapons I am the thunderbolt, among cows I am the wish-fulfilling cow, among living beings I am Kamadeva, and among serpents I am Vasuki.
Krishna embodies power, abundance, desire, and mystical forces.
God manifests in strength, prosperity, and forces beyond ordinary perception.

Krishna shows His manifestation in the thunderbolt (Vajra), wealth and sustenance (Kamadhuk), love (Kamadeva), and mystical power (Vasuki), emphasizing His omnipresence in all domains of life and nature.
See God in forces of power, abundance, desire, and mystery.
Among the infinite Nagas I am Ananta; among Yadus I am Varuna; among ancestors I am Aryama, and among those who restrain I am Yama.
Krishna manifests in eternity, governance of clans, ancestral order, and law.
Divine presence ensures cosmic order, law, and eternal stability.
Krishna reveals that His manifestations maintain cosmic law, ancestral respect, and eternal balance, demonstrating the divine influence in governance and order.
Respect law, tradition, and cosmic order as expressions of God’s manifestations.
Among the Daityas I am Prahlada, among creatures I am the lion among beasts, and among birds I am Garuda.
Krishna embodies devotion, courage, and freedom.
God’s manifestations reflect virtue, strength, and supremacy in all beings.

Krishna emphasizes His presence in the most devoted (Prahlada), the strongest (lion), and the swiftest or loftiest (Garuda), highlighting that supreme divine qualities are mirrored in exemplary beings across realms.
Seek inspiration from devotion, courage, and excellence as reflections of God.
Among the winds I am the wind, among wielders of weapons I am Rama; among serpents I am Vasuki, and among rivers I am the Ganga.
Krishna manifests in natural forces, heroes, divine serpents, and sacred rivers.
Divine manifestations exist in nature, valor, and sacred elements.

Krishna identifies Himself in powerful and revered entities, showing that the divine pervades both natural forces and exemplary beings. This highlights His omnipresence in all significant aspects of creation.
See God in natural forces, heroic ideals, and sacred elements around you.
I am the beginning, middle, and end of all creation, O Arjuna; I am the knowledge of the self and the debate among scholars.
Krishna pervades all of creation and is the essence of spiritual and intellectual understanding.
God is the foundation and sustaining force of all creation, as well as knowledge itself.

Krishna explains that He is the source, sustainer, and ultimate end of everything, and also the essence of spiritual wisdom and intellectual discourse. This emphasizes His presence in all spheres of life.
Understand that God is the source and support of all creation and knowledge; cultivate reverence in all your actions.
Among letters I am the syllable “A”, among dualities I am conflict, and among the imperishable I am time. I am the creator, all-pervading.
Krishna embodies letters, dualities, time, and omnipresence.
God’s presence spans the fundamental elements of language, duality, and universal existence.

Krishna identifies Himself with fundamental principles: letters of language, opposites in the world, time, and the all-pervading essence of creation. This demonstrates His immanence in both abstract and material realms.
Recognize God in language, life’s dualities, and the passage of time.
I am death, the destroyer of all; I am the source of all that will arise. I am fame, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, and forgiveness.
Krishna embodies both destructive and positive forces.
Divine manifestations include all aspects of existence, from creation to virtues.
Krishna demonstrates that He manifests as both the inevitable forces like death and as virtues such as intelligence, fame, and forgiveness. This reflects the all-encompassing nature of God.
Accept both life’s challenges and virtues as expressions of divine presence.
Among the hymns of the Sama Veda I am the Brihat-Sama; among meters I am the Gayatri; among months I am Margashirsha, and among flowers I am the jasmine.
Krishna pervades sacred hymns, meters, months, and beautiful flowers.
God is reflected in spiritual practices, time, and the beauty of creation.
Krishna shows that sacred chants, poetic meters, time cycles, and fragrant flowers are all His manifestations, highlighting the connection between divinity, worship, and nature.
Respect sacred traditions, nature, and spiritual rituals as expressions of God.
Among gambling I am deceit, among brilliant people I am brilliance; among victors I am victory, among efforts I am enterprise, and among the qualities of beings I am goodness.
Krishna manifests in both virtues and worldly skills or tendencies.
Divine manifestations include both moral qualities and worldly excellence.
Krishna demonstrates His presence in skill, strategy, effort, and virtuous qualities, illustrating that all aspects of life—ethical and practical—bear traces of the divine.
See divine influence in both virtuous conduct and worldly efforts.
Among the Vrishnis I am Vasudeva, among the Pandavas I am Arjuna, among sages I am Vyasa, and among poets I am the sage Ushana.
Krishna manifests in exemplary leaders, heroes, sages, and poets.
God’s presence is reflected in extraordinary beings across generations.
Krishna identifies Himself in great leaders and sages, showing that exemplary individuals are manifestations of divine qualities. Devotion and emulation of such beings strengthen spiritual growth.
Draw inspiration from exemplary leaders, sages, and poets as expressions of divine qualities.
Among punishments I am the rod, among those who control I am discipline, among secretive beings I am silence, and among the learned I am knowledge.
Krishna embodies discipline, control, wisdom, and secrecy.
Divine manifestations guide ethical conduct and the pursuit of knowledge.
Krishna emphasizes that moral order, control, and wisdom originate from the divine. Silence and discretion are also reflections of divine awareness.
Practice discipline, cultivate knowledge, and respect silence as aspects of divine influence.
O Arjuna, I am the seed of all beings. There is nothing in existence, moving or unmoving, that is not contained in Me.
Krishna is the source of all creation, the seed of every being.
Everything in existence has its origin in God.
Krishna asserts that all living and non-living entities emanate from Him. Recognizing God as the ultimate source of all things helps cultivate reverence and surrender.
View all life and matter as originating from God; treat all beings with respect.
There is no end to My divine manifestations, O Arjuna. This has been explained briefly here, but the extent of My opulences is limitless.
God’s glory is boundless and cannot be fully described.
Krishna reveals that His manifestations extend infinitely across creation. Even detailed descriptions can only touch a fraction of His divine opulence.
Contemplate God’s infinite glory to inspire awe, devotion, and humility.
Whatever in creation is glorious, mighty, or beautiful, know that it is a manifestation of My divine energy.
All impressive qualities and powers in the universe reflect God.
Divine energy underlies all beauty, strength, and glory in the world.
Krishna explains that everything remarkable in the universe—strength, beauty, power—is a reflection of His energy. Recognizing this cultivates reverence for life and creation.
Appreciate beauty, strength, and excellence in life as expressions of divine energy.
Or, if it is difficult for you to know all of this, then understand that I, though existing as a part, pervade the entire universe.
Even if individual manifestations are hard to grasp, God pervades all existence.
God’s presence is universal, even if not fully perceived.
Krishna reassures Arjuna that His essence exists everywhere, pervading all creation. Devotion and recognition of His omnipresence is sufficient for spiritual understanding.
Remember that God exists in all beings and things; cultivate awareness of His omnipresence in daily life.

chapter 11.EXPLAINATION: Arjuna said: To understand You, the Supreme Self, You have revealed this most secret knowledge of the soul. O Lord, this delusion of mine is now dispelled.
Arjuna’s doubt and confusion vanish as Krishna imparts supreme knowledge.
Divine guidance removes ignorance and opens the way to true understanding.
Arjuna acknowledges that Krishna’s revelation of the Supreme Self has clarified his mind, removing delusion and confusion. Knowledge of the ultimate reality brings clarity and spiritual insight.
Seek guidance from divine wisdom or enlightened teachers to overcome confusion and ignorance.
I have heard in detail about the birth and dissolution of beings, as well as about Your imperishable glory and majesty, O lotus-eyed Lord.
Arjuna expresses that he has learned about Krishna’s eternal greatness and cosmic role.
Understanding the divine nature and cosmic function of God deepens reverence and faith.
Arjuna acknowledges Krishna’s teachings on the cycle of creation and dissolution and His supreme, eternal majesty. This understanding strengthens devotion and clarity about the cosmos.
Reflect on the divine order of the universe to cultivate humility and reverence.
Thus, O Supreme Lord, I wish to see Your divine, supreme form, O best of men.
Arjuna desires to witness Krishna’s cosmic, all-encompassing form.
True devotion includes the yearning to perceive the divine beyond ordinary appearances.
Arjuna, having gained knowledge, now seeks to experience Krishna’s Vishwaroopa—the universal form that embodies all existence. This reflects the aspiration to witness the infinite within the finite.
Cultivate longing for spiritual experiences and direct perception of the divine.
If it is possible for me to see it, O Lord, then please reveal to me, O Yogeshwara, Your imperishable universal form.
Arjuna requests Krishna to reveal His cosmic form.
Spiritual seekers must actively ask for divine guidance to attain higher realization.

Arjuna humbly requests Krishna to display the Vishwaroopa, acknowledging that the divine form cannot be seen without the Lord’s grace. This highlights the importance of sincere seeking in spiritual growth.
Ask for divine guidance with humility and devotion to witness higher truths.
The Blessed Lord said: Behold, O Arjuna, My forms, hundreds and thousands, of various kinds, divine, with different colors and shapes.
Krishna begins showing His infinite and diverse manifestations to Arjuna.
God’s divine form is limitless and manifests in countless ways.
Krishna demonstrates the Vishwaroopa to Arjuna, showing countless divine forms, each unique. This illustrates the infinite and all-pervading nature of God beyond human comprehension.
Acknowledge and appreciate the infinite aspects of divinity in life and creation.
Behold the Adityas, Vasus, Rudras, Ashvins, Maruts, and many other marvels never seen before, O Bharata.
Krishna’s form encompasses all deities and wondrous manifestations.
The divine form unites all cosmic powers and beings.
Krishna reveals that His cosmic form contains all gods, celestial beings, and miracles. This vision shows that all divine powers originate from a single supreme source.
See divinity in all beings and powers, recognizing their unity in God.
Here, in this one form, behold the entire moving and non-moving universe. O Gudakesha, whatever else you wish to see, behold in My body.
Krishna’s universal form contains all of creation.
Krishna emphasizes that His cosmic form is the embodiment of the entire universe. Devotees should understand that all existence, seen and unseen, is contained within God.
Meditate on the interconnectedness of all life and matter as expressions of God.
But you cannot see Me with your own eyes. Therefore, I give you divine vision. Behold My supreme, divine power.
Divine vision is needed to perceive God’s cosmic form.
Spiritual insight requires grace beyond ordinary perception.
Krishna grants Arjuna divine sight to perceive the Vishwaroopa, teaching that ordinary senses cannot comprehend the infinite. True vision comes through spiritual empowerment.
Seek spiritual insight through devotion and grace to perceive higher truths.
Sanjaya said: Thus addressed, the Supreme Lord of Yoga, Hari, showed Arjuna His supreme divine form.
Krishna reveals His Vishwaroopa to Arjuna.
God reveals His infinite power and majesty to the sincere seeker.
Sanjaya narrates the moment of revelation, where Krishna shows His universal, awe-inspiring form. This emphasizes the importance of faith and devotion in witnessing divine truth.
Remain devoted and receptive to divine experiences; they reveal God’s majesty.
With many faces and eyes, marvelous to behold, adorned with countless divine ornaments, and holding many uplifted weapons.
Krishna’s Vishwaroopa is magnificent, multi-faceted, and awe-inspiring.
The divine form reflects infinite power, beauty, and majesty.
Krishna’s universal form is described as awe-inspiring, with countless faces, eyes, ornaments, and weapons, symbolizing His omnipotence and the boundless nature of His energy.
Contemplate the divine majesty in all creation to inspire awe, devotion, and humility.
He wore a divine garland, divine garments, and anointed Himself with divine fragrances. Everything about Him was wondrous, infinite, and all-pervading.
Krishna’s cosmic form is adorned with divine decorations and radiates awe-inspiring majesty.
Divinity is expressed through splendor, majesty, and the infinite nature of God.

Arjuna observes Krishna’s Vishwaroopa, noting the divine ornaments, garments, and fragrances that make His form beyond ordinary perception. This emphasizes the transcendent beauty and majesty of the Supreme Being.
Appreciate the divine qualities in the world and seek to perceive the extraordinary in the ordinary.
If a thousand suns were to rise together in the sky, their brilliance could not compare to the radiance of that Supreme Being.
Krishna’s cosmic form shines far beyond any natural source of light.
The divine surpasses all worldly measures of power and brilliance.
Arjuna compares the overwhelming radiance of Krishna’s form to a thousand suns, emphasizing that God’s power and splendor are infinite and beyond human comprehension.
Recognize the limitless power and glory of the divine in all forms of life.
In that form, Arjuna saw the entire universe divided in many ways, all contained in the one body of the Supreme God.
Krishna’s Vishwaroopa contains the entire universe in a single form.
The infinite universe is encompassed in the divine, showing unity in multiplicity.
Arjuna perceives all creation—the heavens, the earth, beings, and elements—within Krishna’s body, highlighting the oneness behind the diversity of existence.
Understand the unity of all creation in God; respect all beings as manifestations of the divine.
Then Arjuna, overwhelmed with wonder and his hair standing on end, bowed to the Lord with folded hands and spoke respectfully.
Arjuna reacts to the awe-inspiring cosmic form with reverence and humility.
Encountering the divine inspires humility, reverence, and devotion.
Seeing Krishna’s infinite form, Arjuna is awed and responds with a gesture of devotion. This teaches that spiritual encounters evoke both amazement and deep respect.
Cultivate reverence and humility when contemplating divine or extraordinary experiences.
Arjuna said: I see all the gods in Your body, O Lord, as well as all beings and sages, Brahma, the great Lord, all serpents, and divine creatures.
Krishna’s body contains all gods, beings, and sages.
God is the source and embodiment of all beings, divine and mortal.
Arjuna observes the presence of all divine and celestial beings within Krishna’s universal form, signifying that all powers and life are expressions of the Supreme.
Respect all life and divinity as expressions of God.
I see You with many arms, faces, and eyes, infinite in every direction. There is no end, no middle, no beginning to Your form, O Lord of the universe.
Krishna’s cosmic form is infinite, beyond beginning or end.
The divine is boundless and cannot be fully comprehended by the human mind.
Arjuna perceives Krishna’s form as limitless, without a clear beginning or end. This conveys the incomprehensible and infinite nature of God.
Develop awareness of God’s infinity to cultivate humility and devotion.
Crowned, holding the mace and discus, radiating immense brilliance, I see You shining with flames and the light of the sun everywhere, unmeasurable and impossible to look at directly.
Krishna’s Vishwaroopa is adorned with weapons and shines with unbearable radiance.
Divine power and majesty surpass ordinary human capacity to perceive.
Arjuna describes Krishna’s awe-inspiring form with weapons and divine radiance that is impossible to behold directly, reflecting omnipotence and glory.
Respect the overwhelming majesty of the divine and approach spiritual experiences with care and humility.
You are the imperishable, the ultimate reality, the supreme repository of the universe, eternal and protector of dharma. You are the Supreme Person, in my view.
Krishna embodies the eternal, imperishable essence and the protector of righteousness.
God is eternal, the ultimate source, and the guardian of dharma.
Arjuna identifies Krishna as the eternal, imperishable, supreme being who upholds the universe and protects cosmic order, emphasizing His role as the ultimate reality.
Seek to align with the eternal principles of righteousness upheld by God.
Without beginning, middle, or end, endowed with infinite energy, countless arms, and moon and sun-like eyes, I see You with radiant faces, heating the universe with Your own brilliance.
Krishna’s cosmic form is radiant, infinite, and all-powerful.
The divine is omnipotent, infinite, and all-pervading.
Arjuna perceives Krishna’s form as an infinite source of energy and light, sustaining and illuminating the universe. This underscores God’s limitless power and omnipresence.
Recognize divine energy as the source of strength and illumination in life
Heaven and earth, and all between them, are pervaded by You alone, in every direction. Seeing Your terrifying form, the three worlds trembled, O mighty one.
Krishna’s form encompasses all worlds and inspires awe and fear.
Divine omnipresence includes the power to inspire reverence and respect across all creation.
Krishna’s Vishwaroopa fills all directions and the entire cosmos. The sheer power of His form causes even the universe to tremble, emphasizing the awe-inspiring majesty of God.
Approach life with respect for the divine power that sustains the universe.
Some gods enter You in awe, while others, fearful, bow with folded hands and say “All hail!” The great perfected sages praise You abundantly with hymns and devotion.
Even celestial beings and sages show reverence and awe before Krishna’s cosmic form.
Divine majesty inspires respect, worship, and humility among all beings.

Krishna’s Vishwaroopa evokes awe and devotion from gods, sages, and perfected beings alike. The cosmic form surpasses ordinary comprehension, demonstrating the universal acknowledgment of divine supremacy.
Approach the divine with reverence and recognize that awe and respect are natural responses to supreme power.
The Rudras, Adityas, Vasus, Sadhyas, Ashvins, Maruts, Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas all gaze at You, astonished and amazed.
All celestial beings and divine forces are mesmerized by Krishna’s cosmic form.
The Vishwaroopa embodies all cosmic powers, leaving even the most exalted beings amazed.
Arjuna observes that Krishna’s form captivates every divine being, showing the unity and totality of all powers in the Supreme. This reflects that God is the source of all celestial energies.
Recognize that all powers, visible or invisible, originate from the divine, fostering respect for creation
O mighty-armed Lord, Your form is vast, with many faces, eyes, and arms. Your many mouths and fierce teeth strike terror, and seeing this, all beings tremble.
Krishna’s cosmic form is enormous and terrifying, symbolizing immense power.
The divine can appear fearsome, reflecting the boundless and awe-inspiring nature of God.
The many arms, mouths, and eyes of Krishna’s Vishwaroopa display His supreme might. The fearsome aspects symbolize the cosmic power that maintains universal order and destroys evil.
Respect divine power, even when it appears overwhelming or formidable.
His form touched the sky, with multicolored radiance and vast, brilliant eyes. Seeing You, O Vishnu, my inner being trembles; I find no calm or composure.
The cosmic form overwhelms Arjuna, evoking awe and inner disturbance.
Encountering the infinite divine may overwhelm the senses, yet it inspires devotion.
Arjuna is unable to maintain composure in Krishna’s presence. The multicolored brilliance and expansive eyes illustrate the incomprehensible majesty of God.
Accept that true encounters with divinity can be overwhelming, and cultivate humility and surrender.
Your fierce teeth resemble blazing fire. I cannot determine directions or maintain composure. Have mercy, O Lord of the Universe.
Krishna’s form is terrifying, and Arjuna seeks divine mercy to maintain his resolve.
Even devotees must seek grace when confronting the overwhelming power of God.
The fierce aspect of the Vishwaroopa reminds Arjuna of the destructive power of divinity. Devotees rely on God’s mercy to remain steadfast.
Seek divine guidance and protection when facing overwhelming challenges in life.
All sons of Dhritarashtra, along with their armies, Bhishma, Drona, Karna, and the chief warriors of my side, are entering Your mouths, O Lord.
Krishna’s cosmic form is consuming all beings impartially, showing the inevitability of cosmic law.
Divine power governs life and death impartially, beyond human distinctions.
Arjuna witnesses the impartial and all-encompassing nature of Krishna’s form. This symbolizes that cosmic law operates beyond favoritism or attachment.
Understand that life’s outcomes are governed by universal law, and cultivate equanimity.
Some faces of Yours devour swiftly, with terrifying teeth. Others appear shattered and scattered.
Different aspects of the cosmic form display varied destructive powers.
Divine manifestations include both protective and destructive energies.
Krishna’s cosmic form has multiple facets, some consuming, some awe-inspiring. This represents the multifaceted nature of divine power—creation, preservation, and destruction.
Recognize the balance of forces in life and the universe; accept both creation and dissolution.
Just as many river streams flow into the ocean, so do the heroic warriors of the human world enter Your blazing mouths.
All beings are ultimately absorbed by the divine, like rivers merging into the ocean.
All life returns to its divine source; individual identity is temporary.
Krishna’s cosmic form demonstrates the ultimate absorption of all beings into the Supreme, symbolizing the impermanence of the material world and the inevitability of divine union.
Focus on spiritual union with God rather than attachment to temporary identities.
Just as moths are drawn to a blazing fire, so too do mortals rush into Your mouths with irresistible force.
Mortals, drawn by karma and fate, are consumed by the cosmic will.
Humans are bound by destiny and the divine plan, like moths to a flame.
Arjuna observes the inevitability of divine justice: beings are drawn toward outcomes ordained by the cosmic law, illustrating surrender to the divine will.
Live with awareness of karmic consequences and align actions with dharma.
You devour the entire universe with blazing mouths, and Your terrifying radiance fills all directions, O Vishnu.
Krishna’s cosmic form consumes and illuminates the universe with boundless power.
The divine is omnipresent, omnipotent, and all-consuming in its cosmic operation.
The terrifying and radiant Vishwaroopa illustrates the supreme power and universality of God, capable of consuming and sustaining all creation simultaneously.
Acknowledge the omnipotence of God and cultivate surrender, humility, and reverence in life.
Arjuna said: Tell me, O Lord, who You are in this form of Your mercy. May it be auspicious for me. I wish to know You, for I do not know Your manifestations.
Arjuna seeks clarity about Krishna’s cosmic form, eager to understand His divine nature.
Spiritual knowledge begins with humility and the desire to understand the divine.
Overwhelmed by Krishna’s Vishwaroopa, Arjuna requests an explanation of His form. This shows that devotion coupled with inquiry leads to understanding.
Never hesitate to ask questions in spiritual or life pursuits; genuine curiosity leads to clarity.
Krishna said: I am Time, the mighty destroyer of worlds, come to annihilate all beings. Even without you, all the warriors on both sides will not survive.
Krishna reveals Himself as the cosmic force of Time, which governs life and death.
Time and cosmic law are supreme, and all beings are subject to their power.
Krishna identifies Himself with Time, emphasizing that the destruction in battle is part of cosmic law. Human efforts cannot resist the ordained course of events.
Understand the impermanence of life and work in alignment with dharma, accepting outcomes beyond personal control.
Arise, achieve glory, and conquer your enemies. Possess the prosperous kingdom. Those slain by Me are already dead; you are only the instrument.
Krishna urges Arjuna to act, assuring him that the results are already determined.
Perform your duty without attachment, knowing that the divine guides outcomes.
Krishna emphasizes that Arjuna is an instrument of divine will. Action must be performed courageously while surrendering results to God.
Focus on diligent effort, not fear of results, and trust in the larger cosmic plan.
Fight and destroy Drona, Bhishma, Jayadratha, Karna, and all other brave warriors who oppose you. Do not grieve; you will conquer them in battle.
Krishna reassures Arjuna that victory is assured; he must act without hesitation.
Courage and action aligned with duty lead to success.
Krishna directs Arjuna to act decisively, showing that duty must be performed with determination and detachment from fear or sorrow.
Face challenges boldly and perform responsibilities without being paralyzed by fear.
Sanjaya said: Hearing Krishna’s words, Arjuna, with folded hands and trembling, bowed to Krishna in reverence.
Arjuna responds with humility and devotion upon hearing Krishna’s divine instruction.
Divine guidance inspires respect, humility, and surrender.
Even a great warrior like Arjuna reacts with awe and reverence when confronted with supreme wisdom and divine form.
Approach guidance and wisdom with respect and a receptive mind.
Arjuna said: O Lord, seeing Your glory, the world rejoices. Beings tremble in fear, all directions are in awe, and even the Siddhas bow to You.
Krishna’s majesty inspires joy, reverence, and fear simultaneously.
Divine presence commands respect and devotion from all beings.
The cosmic form evokes mixed responses—joy among devotees, fear among the fearful, and universal respect—highlighting the multifaceted power of God.
Recognize that true greatness evokes both admiration and reverence.
Why should anyone not bow to You, O mighty one, the inexhaustible Lord, the indestructible, supreme being?
Krishna’s supremacy makes Him worthy of universal veneration.
The divine commands devotion universally due to omnipotence and transcendence.
Arjuna marvels that Krishna, being the ultimate, indestructible, and infinite Lord, deserves homage from all beings without exception.
Respect the supreme in all forms, acknowledging God’s omnipotence and universality.
You are the primal God, the Supreme Person, the ancient one, the ultimate refuge of the universe. You know everything and the entire cosmos manifests from You.
Krishna is the eternal source and ruler of the universe.
God is the origin, sustainer, and knower of all.
Krishna explains His eternal and all-encompassing nature, making it clear that all creation and knowledge stem from Him.
Seek alignment with God as the source and guide of all existence.
I bow to You repeatedly, O primal God, the wind, Yama, Agni, Varuna, the moon, Prajapati, and the forefathers.
Arjuna offers repeated salutations to the Supreme, recognizing divinity in multiple forms.
The divine manifests in multiple forms, all worthy of respect.
Arjuna acknowledges that Krishna embodies cosmic powers, natural forces, and ancestral divinity, showing the interconnectedness of the divine.
Honor divinity in all forms—natural, cosmic, and spiritual.
I bow to You from all directions, front, back, and sides. You possess infinite valor and unmatched prowess, and by this, You encompass all.
Krishna is omnipresent, infinitely powerful, and encompasses everything.
The supreme being is omnipotent, omnipresent, and omniscient.
Arjuna expresses full surrender, recognizing Krishna’s infinite strength, courage, and supremacy over all existence.
Surrender to the divine omnipotence and cultivate reverence for God’s all-encompassing power.
Arjuna said: O Krishna, O friend, O Yadava, addressing You as “friend,” I unintentionally expressed familiarity, unaware of Your greatness. Please forgive my presumption born of love.
Arjuna apologizes for speaking casually, acknowledging Krishna’s supreme stature.
Even in devotion, humility and awareness of the divine’s greatness are essential.
Arjuna’s love made him address Krishna familiarly, but he immediately recognizes his mistake. This shows that devotion should be tempered with respect for divine majesty.
Balance love and reverence in relationships with the divine and in life; humility enhances devotion.
If I have unintentionally treated You lightly in sports, sleep, or meals, or elsewhere, I humbly seek forgiveness from You, O Infinite One.
Arjuna seeks pardon for any casual behavior that may have seemed disrespectful.
Acknowledging mistakes and seeking forgiveness strengthens spiritual and personal relationships.
Even minor acts, if done without awareness, can be disrespectful. Arjuna demonstrates self-awareness and humility in devotion.
Practice mindfulness in actions toward those we respect, and apologize when necessary.
You are the father of all worlds, the moving and unmoving, revered as the greatest teacher. There is none equal to You, nor anyone else with unmatched influence over the three worlds.
Arjuna praises Krishna’s unparalleled status as the Supreme Being and cosmic ruler.
Divine supremacy encompasses all creation, knowledge, and influence.
Arjuna recognizes Krishna as the ultimate authority and origin of all, surpassing even the greatest teachers and sages.
Recognize the supreme in all endeavors and acknowledge ultimate sources of wisdom
Therefore, I bow to You, offer my obeisance, and surrender my body, seeking Your grace. O Lord, You are as dear to me as a father, as a friend, and as the beloved is to the beloved.
Arjuna expresses complete surrender and devotion to Krishna.
Surrender, devotion, and reverence are core aspects of spiritual practice.
Arjuna demonstrates total surrender, showing the multiple dimensions of devotion—love, respect, and friendship.
Cultivate surrender and devotion in spiritual or meaningful human relationships.
I am overwhelmed with joy and fear at seeing this unprecedented form. O Lord of the universe, please be gracious and show me Your divine form in a calm manner.
Arjuna experiences awe, fear, and amazement at Krishna’s cosmic form.
Encountering the divine directly evokes mixed emotions; grace helps in understanding and acceptance.
Arjuna is both terrified and delighted, illustrating the overwhelming majesty of the cosmic form and the need for divine grace to comprehend it.
Approach awe-inspiring experiences with humility and seek guidance to understand them calmly.
I wish to see You with a crown, holding mace and disc, and also in the form of four-armed, thousand-armed Vishwaroopa.
Arjuna requests to witness both the familiar and cosmic forms of Krishna.
Spiritual seekers may aspire to perceive both personal and cosmic dimensions of the divine.
Arjuna desires a comprehensive vision of Krishna, recognizing both the approachable personal form and the awe-inspiring universal form.
Seek deeper understanding of reality by observing both the approachable and expansive aspects of life.
Krishna said: Arjuna, I have shown this supreme, fiery, infinite cosmic form to You, which no one else has ever seen.
Krishna reveals His Vishwaroopa exclusively to Arjuna.
Divine revelation is unique and often requires readiness and devotion.
Krishna emphasizes the uniqueness of this vision and that such divine insight is not easily accessible, highlighting the special connection with devoted seekers.
Value and honor unique spiritual insights and experiences; they come to those prepared.
No sacrifices, Vedic study, donations, austerities, or rituals could show this form to men in the mortal world.
Krishna’s cosmic form cannot be attained by ordinary rituals or scholarly effort alone.
Direct divine experience transcends ritualistic practice and requires grace.
Krishna emphasizes that His cosmic vision is beyond human means and is revealed only through divine grace and devotion.
Understand that some truths are beyond external effort and need inner readiness and grace.
Do not be distressed or bewildered, having seen this terrible form. Remain composed and look again with a calm mind.
Krishna reassures Arjuna not to be afraid of the terrifying cosmic form.
Divine experiences may seem overwhelming; composure is essential to comprehend them.
Krishna comforts Arjuna, highlighting the need for emotional stability to witness the divine fully.
Maintain calmness and composure when facing overwhelming or extraordinary experiences.
Sanjaya said: Having spoken thus, Krishna showed His own form again to Arjuna, calming his fear and restoring his gentle, peaceful nature.
Krishna returns to His familiar, serene form, reassuring Arjuna.
Divine grace restores peace after awe-inspiring experiences.
After revealing the cosmic form, Krishna reassures Arjuna with His familiar form, demonstrating both the awe and mercy of the divine.
Trust that guidance and reassurance follow moments of intense challenge or revelation.
Arjuna said: Seeing Your human form, O gentle Janardana, I have regained composure and returned to my normal consciousness.
After witnessing the terrifying cosmic form, Arjuna feels calm and returns to his ordinary perception.
Exposure to the divine in its cosmic form can overwhelm, but returning to the personal form restores composure.

Arjuna had been awe-struck and fearful at the universal form. Now, seeing Krishna in His familiar human form, he is reassured and regains normal consciousness, showing the balance between divine majesty and accessibility.
In overwhelming situations, grounding oneself and returning to familiar, reassuring contexts restores calm and clarity.
Krishna said: O Arjuna, you have seen a form that is difficult to behold, which even the gods long to see regularly.
Krishna emphasizes the rarity and magnificence of the cosmic vision.
Some divine truths are so extraordinary that even celestial beings yearn to witness them.
The cosmic form is beyond ordinary comprehension. Krishna reminds Arjuna that he has been granted a rare and exceptional glimpse of the divine, underscoring the importance of grace.
Recognize and cherish rare opportunities and insights, understanding their uniqueness.
This form of Mine cannot be perceived through the Vedas, austerities, charity, or rituals. Yet you have seen Me in this way, O Arjuna.
Direct experience of the divine transcends scholarly study and ritualistic practice.
